text: This animation visualizes gradient descent on a two-dimensional loss surface.
The surface represents the loss function f of x y equals x squared plus y squared, a convex bowl shape.
Yellow contour lines show constant loss values, helping visualize the landscape.
The red dot marks our starting position, with a shadow showing its projection.
Watch as gradient descent iteratively moves the point downhill, following the negative gradient.
Each step reduces the loss value, and the step size adapts to the gradient magnitude.
Notice how the path becomes shorter as we approach the minimum, where the gradient is smaller.
The algorithm converges to the minimum at the origin, where both x and y approach zero.
Green indicates convergence. Gradient descent successfully found the global minimum of the convex function.
